title: N001: Scaffold Helm chart with Chart.yaml and values.yaml
description:
**Size:** 🏗️ large
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** critical
**Source:** Proposed for 'Helm Chart Packaging

Chart structure under charts/'

Create the Helm chart foundation under charts/memcached-operator/. Includes: Chart.yaml (type: application, appVersion tracking operator version), comprehensive values.yaml with all tunables (image.repository/tag, replicaCount, resources, webhook.enabled, certmanager.enabled, serviceMonitor.enabled, networkPolicy.enabled, watchNamespaces, leaderElection.enabled, rbac.create, crds.managedByHelm), and templates/_helpers.tpl with standard label/name/selector helpers matching existing app.kubernetes.io/name conventions from config/. Also includes the CRD in crds/ directory (copied from config/crd/bases/) plus the optional templates/crd-template.yaml gated by crds.managedByHelm for helm-upgrade-managed CRD updates.

**Rationale:** Foundation that all other features depend on. The values.yaml defines the contract for all templates, _helpers.tpl provides reusable template functions, and CRD handling (both crds/ directory and optional template) must be established first since it affects how the operator is installed and upgraded.

**Affected Areas:**
- charts/memcached-operator/Chart.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/values.yaml
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl
- charts/memcached-operator/crds/
- charts/memcached-operator/templates/crd-template.yaml

stories.title: Operator deployer installs chart with helm install
stories.want: to install the memcached-operator via `helm install` using a well-structured chart with sensible defaults
stories.so_that: I can deploy the operator without manually assembling Kustomize overlays
criteria: Chart.yaml exists at charts/memcached-operator/Chart.yaml with type: application and apiVersion: v2
Chart.yaml appVersion field tracks the current operator version from Makefile/Dockerfile (e.g. 0.1.0)
values.yaml provides defaults for image.repository (controller), image.tag (latest), replicaCount (1), resources (matching config/manager/manager.yaml limits)
helm lint passes without errors or warnings on the chart directory
helm template renders valid YAML for a default install (no values overrides)

stories.title: Operator deployer customizes all deployment tunables via values.yaml
stories.want: a comprehensive values.yaml with all operator tunables documented with comments
stories.so_that: I can customize the operator deployment without reading source code
criteria: values.yaml contains keys: image.repository, image.tag, image.pullPolicy, replicaCount, resources.limits, resources.requests
values.yaml contains toggle keys: webhook.enabled (true), certmanager.enabled (true), serviceMonitor.enabled (false), networkPolicy.enabled (false)
values.yaml contains keys: watchNamespaces (empty list), leaderElection.enabled (true), rbac.create (true), crds.managedByHelm (false)
values.yaml contains serviceAccount.create (true), serviceAccount.name (empty string), serviceAccount.annotations (empty map)
Each section in values.yaml has YAML comments explaining the purpose and valid values

stories.title: Template author uses consistent helpers for labels and names
stories.want: _helpers.tpl to provide standard name, fullname, labels, and selectorLabels helpers
stories.so_that: all templates use consistent naming and labeling matching existing app.kubernetes.io conventions
criteria: _helpers.tpl defines memcached-operator.name returning chart name (truncated to 63 chars)
_helpers.tpl defines memcached-operator.fullname returning release-prefixed name (truncated to 63 chars)
_helpers.tpl defines memcached-operator.labels returning app.kubernetes.io/name, app.kubernetes.io/instance, app.kubernetes.io/version, app.kubernetes.io/managed-by labels
_helpers.tpl defines memcached-operator.selectorLabels returning app.kubernetes.io/name and app.kubernetes.io/instance
Label helpers match the convention in config/ manifests (app.kubernetes.io/name: memcached-operator)

stories.title: Operator deployer gets CRD installed automatically on first install
stories.want: the Memcached CRD to be installed automatically when running helm install
stories.so_that: I don't need to manually apply CRDs before installing the chart
criteria: crds/ directory exists at charts/memcached-operator/crds/
crds/ contains memcached.c5c3.io_memcacheds.yaml copied from config/crd/bases/
CRD file is valid and matches the generated CRD from controller-gen
helm install creates the CRD before other resources (Helm native crds/ behavior)

stories.title: Operator deployer opts in to Helm-managed CRD upgrades
stories.want: to optionally have helm upgrade manage CRD updates via crds.managedByHelm toggle
stories.so_that: I can choose between manual CRD upgrades and automated helm-managed upgrades
criteria: When crds.managedByHelm is false (default), no CRD template is rendered — CRD only in crds/ directory
When crds.managedByHelm is true, templates/crd-template.yaml renders the CRD as a regular template
The CRD template content matches the CRD in crds/ directory
helm template with crds.managedByHelm=true outputs the CRD resource in rendered templates

stories.title: Operator deployer validates chart correctness with helm-unittest
stories.want: helm-unittest test suites that verify default rendering and value toggles
stories.so_that: chart changes are validated before release and regressions are caught
criteria: Test files exist under charts/memcached-operator/tests/
Tests verify Chart.yaml metadata (name, apiVersion, type)
Tests verify values.yaml defaults produce expected rendered output
Tests verify crds.managedByHelm toggle controls CRD template rendering
Tests verify _helpers.tpl label outputs match expected app.kubernetes.io labels

stories.title: Chart author maintains documentation for chart installation
stories.want: reference documentation for Chart.yaml, values.yaml tunables, and _helpers.tpl
stories.so_that: I understand all available configuration options before installing
criteria: Reference doc exists at docs/reference/backend/helm-chart-scaffold.md
Doc lists all values.yaml keys with types, defaults, and descriptions
Doc explains CRD handling strategy (crds/ directory vs crds.managedByHelm)
Doc describes _helpers.tpl template functions and their outputs

requirements.description: The chart SHALL contain a valid Chart.yaml with apiVersion v2, type application, name memcached-operator, and appVersion tracking the operator version
requirements.rationale: Chart.yaml is the mandatory entry point for any Helm chart; apiVersion v2 is required for Helm 3

scenarios.name: Chart.yaml is valid Helm chart metadata
scenarios.when: helm lint is run on charts/memcached-operator/
scenarios.then: no errors are reported about Chart.yaml

scenarios.name: appVersion reflects operator version
scenarios.when: Chart.yaml is inspected
scenarios.then: appVersion field is set to a semantic version string (e.g. 0.1.0)
and_then: version field is also set to a semantic version (chart version)

scenarios.name: Chart.yaml has required metadata
scenarios.when: Chart.yaml is parsed
scenarios.then: description, home, and sources fields are present
and_then: home URL points to project repository

requirements.description: The chart SHALL contain a comprehensive values.yaml with all operator tunables, each with sensible defaults matching existing config/ manifests
requirements.rationale: values.yaml defines the contract for all templates and must cover every configurable aspect of the operator deployment

scenarios.name: Image configuration defaults match existing setup
scenarios.when: values.yaml is loaded with no overrides
scenarios.then: image.repository defaults to controller, image.tag defaults to latest, image.pullPolicy defaults to IfNotPresent

scenarios.name: Resource defaults match config/manager/manager.yaml
scenarios.when: values.yaml is loaded with no overrides
scenarios.then: resources.limits.cpu=500m, resources.limits.memory=128Mi, resources.requests.cpu=10m, resources.requests.memory=64Mi

scenarios.name: Toggle defaults are appropriate for production
scenarios.when: values.yaml is loaded with no overrides
scenarios.then: webhook.enabled=true, certmanager.enabled=true, leaderElection.enabled=true, rbac.create=true, serviceMonitor.enabled=false, networkPolicy.enabled=false, crds.managedByHelm=false

scenarios.name: All tunables have YAML comments
scenarios.when: values.yaml file is inspected
scenarios.then: every top-level section has a YAML comment explaining its purpose

requirements.description: The chart SHALL contain a _helpers.tpl with name, fullname, chart, labels, and selectorLabels template functions following Helm best practices
requirements.rationale: _helpers.tpl provides DRY label/name generation used by all templates; must match existing app.kubernetes.io label conventions

scenarios.name: Name helper truncates to 63 characters
scenarios.when: memcached-operator.name is called
scenarios.then: it returns the chart name or nameOverride, truncated to 63 characters with trailing hyphens trimmed

scenarios.name: Fullname helper uses release name
scenarios.when: memcached-operator.fullname is called with release name my-release
scenarios.then: it returns my-release-memcached-operator truncated to 63 characters
and_then: if fullnameOverride is set, it returns that value instead

scenarios.name: Labels helper returns standard Kubernetes labels
scenarios.when: memcached-operator.labels is called
scenarios.then: output includes helm.sh/chart, app.kubernetes.io/name, app.kubernetes.io/instance, app.kubernetes.io/version, app.kubernetes.io/managed-by

scenarios.name: SelectorLabels helper returns minimal label set
scenarios.when: memcached-operator.selectorLabels is called
scenarios.then: output includes only app.kubernetes.io/name and app.kubernetes.io/instance
and_then: these labels are a subset of the full labels

requirements.description: The chart SHALL include the CRD in crds/ directory for automatic installation on first helm install
requirements.rationale: Helm's crds/ directory convention ensures CRDs are installed before other resources on first install

scenarios.name: CRD file exists in crds/ directory
scenarios.when: charts/memcached-operator/crds/ is listed
scenarios.then: memcached.c5c3.io_memcacheds.yaml is present

scenarios.name: CRD is valid Kubernetes resource
scenarios.when: the CRD YAML is parsed
scenarios.then: it has apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1 and kind: CustomResourceDefinition

scenarios.name: CRD is not rendered as template by default
scenarios.when: helm template is run with default values
scenarios.then: CRD does not appear in rendered template output

requirements.description: The chart SHALL include an optional CRD template at templates/crd-template.yaml gated by crds.managedByHelm
requirements.rationale: Enables helm upgrade to manage CRD lifecycle for users who prefer automated CRD updates

scenarios.name: CRD template not rendered when disabled
scenarios.when: helm template is run with crds.managedByHelm=false
scenarios.then: no CRD resource appears in the rendered output

scenarios.name: CRD template rendered when enabled
scenarios.when: helm template is run with crds.managedByHelm=true
scenarios.then: the CRD resource appears in rendered template output
and_then: CRD has kind: CustomResourceDefinition and correct spec.group

scenarios.name: CRD template has standard labels
scenarios.when: crds.managedByHelm=true and helm template is run
scenarios.then: the rendered CRD includes standard chart labels from _helpers.tpl

requirements.description: The chart SHALL pass helm lint with no errors under default, all-enabled, and minimal configurations
requirements.rationale: helm lint validates chart structure and template syntax; all valid value combinations must lint cleanly

scenarios.name: Default values pass lint
scenarios.when: helm lint charts/memcached-operator/ is run
scenarios.then: exit code is 0 with no ERROR-level messages

scenarios.name: Lint with all toggles enabled passes
scenarios.when: helm lint is run with all optional features enabled
scenarios.then: exit code is 0 with no ERROR-level messages

scenarios.name: Lint with minimal config passes
scenarios.when: helm lint is run with webhook.enabled=false, certmanager.enabled=false, rbac.create=false
scenarios.then: exit code is 0 with no ERROR-level messages

requirements.description: The _helpers.tpl labels SHOULD match existing app.kubernetes.io label conventions from config/ manifests
requirements.rationale: Consistency between Kustomize and Helm deployments prevents confusion and ensures monitoring compatibility

scenarios.name: app.kubernetes.io/name matches existing convention
scenarios.when: selectorLabels are generated
scenarios.then: app.kubernetes.io/name is set to the chart name (memcached-operator)

scenarios.name: app.kubernetes.io/managed-by reflects Helm
scenarios.when: full labels are generated
scenarios.then: app.kubernetes.io/managed-by is Helm

scenarios.name: Chart version label is present
scenarios.when: full labels are generated
scenarios.then: helm.sh/chart label includes chart name and version

requirements.description: The chart SHALL include helm-unittest test suites validating default rendering, CRD toggle, and helper labels
requirements.rationale: Automated tests prevent regressions when modifying chart templates and values

scenarios.name: Default rendering test passes
scenarios.when: helm-unittest runs the default rendering test suite
scenarios.then: all assertions pass for default values rendering

scenarios.name: CRD toggle test passes
scenarios.when: helm-unittest runs the CRD toggle test suite
scenarios.then: assertions confirm CRD template is absent when false and present when true

scenarios.name: Helper label test passes
scenarios.when: helm-unittest runs the helpers test suite
scenarios.then: assertions confirm correct app.kubernetes.io labels are generated

requirements.description: The chart SHALL include a .helmignore file excluding development and CI artifacts from packaging
requirements.rationale: Prevents unnecessary files from bloating the chart archive

scenarios.name: .helmignore excludes common artifacts
scenarios.when: .helmignore is inspected
scenarios.then: it excludes .git, .gitignore, *.swp, .DS_Store, tests/

scenarios.name: helm package respects .helmignore
scenarios.when: helm package is run
scenarios.then: the resulting .tgz does not contain excluded files

requirements.description: Reference documentation SHALL describe all Chart.yaml fields, values.yaml tunables, _helpers.tpl functions, and CRD handling strategy
requirements.rationale: Users need documentation to understand chart configuration without reading template source

scenarios.name: Reference doc exists and is complete
scenarios.when: docs/reference/backend/helm-chart-scaffold.md is opened
scenarios.then: it contains sections for Chart.yaml, values.yaml, _helpers.tpl, and CRD strategy
and_then: all values.yaml keys are documented with type, default, and description

scenarios.name: CRD strategy is explained
scenarios.when: the CRD handling section is read
scenarios.then: it explains crds/ directory vs crds.managedByHelm behavior

tasks.title: Create Chart.yaml with metadata, appVersion, and description (REQ-001)
tasks.description: Create charts/memcached-operator/Chart.yaml with apiVersion: v2, name: memcached-operator, type: application, version: 0.1.0, appVersion: 0.1.0, description, home (https://github.com/c5c3/memcached-operator), sources list, and maintainers. The appVersion should track the operator version. Follow Helm chart metadata conventions.

tasks.title: Create .helmignore file with standard exclusions (REQ-009)
tasks.description: Create charts/memcached-operator/.helmignore with standard exclusions: .git, .gitignore, .bzr, .bzrignore, .hg, .hgignore, .svn, *.swp, .DS_Store, *.tmp, tests/, ci/. Follow standard Helm .helmignore patterns.

tasks.title: Create comprehensive values.yaml with all tunables and YAML comments (REQ-002)
tasks.description: Create charts/memcached-operator/values.yaml with all tunables documented with YAML comments. Sections: image (repository: controller, tag: latest, pullPolicy: IfNotPresent), replicaCount: 1, resources (matching config/manager/manager.yaml: limits cpu 500m memory 128Mi, requests cpu 10m memory 64Mi), nameOverride/fullnameOverride, serviceAccount (create: true, annotations: {}, name: ""), rbac (create: true), leaderElection (enabled: true), webhook (enabled: true), certmanager (enabled: true), serviceMonitor (enabled: false), networkPolicy (enabled: false), watchNamespaces: [], crds (managedByHelm: false), securityContext and podSecurityContext matching config/manager/manager.yaml (runAsNonRoot: true, runAsUser: 65532, seccompProfile RuntimeDefault, container: allowPrivilegeEscalation false, readOnlyRootFilesystem true, capabilities drop ALL), nodeSelector: {}, tolerations: [], affinity: {}. Each section with explanatory comments.

tasks.title: Create _helpers.tpl with name, fullname, chart, labels, and selectorLabels helpers (REQ-003, REQ-007)
tasks.description: Create charts/memcached-operator/templates/_helpers.tpl with standard Helm helpers: (1) memcached-operator.name — returns .Chart.Name or nameOverride truncated to 63 chars; (2) memcached-operator.fullname — returns release-prefixed name or fullnameOverride truncated to 63 chars; (3) memcached-operator.chart — returns chart name-version; (4) memcached-operator.labels — returns labels: helm.sh/chart, app.kubernetes.io/name (from name helper), app.kubernetes.io/instance (.Release.Name), app.kubernetes.io/version (.Chart.AppVersion), app.kubernetes.io/managed-by (.Release.Service); (5) memcached-operator.selectorLabels — returns only app.kubernetes.io/name and app.kubernetes.io/instance; (6) memcached-operator.serviceAccountName — returns serviceAccount.name or fullname. Match existing app.kubernetes.io conventions from config/ manifests.

tasks.title: Copy CRD to crds/ directory for Helm-native install (REQ-004)
tasks.description: Create charts/memcached-operator/crds/ directory and copy config/crd/bases/memcached.c5c3.io_memcacheds.yaml into it. The CRD file must be an exact copy of the generated CRD. Verify the CRD has apiVersion: apiextensions.k8s.io/v1 and kind: CustomResourceDefinition with spec.group: memcached.c5c3.io.

tasks.title: Create CRD template gated by crds.managedByHelm (REQ-005)
tasks.description: Create charts/memcached-operator/templates/crd-template.yaml. Wrap the entire CRD content in {{- if .Values.crds.managedByHelm }} / {{- end }}. The CRD content should match the file in crds/ directory but with added metadata.labels from {{ include "memcached-operator.labels" . | nindent 4 }}. Add a comment at top explaining this is for helm-managed CRD upgrades and is disabled by default.

tasks.title: Create empty templates/NOTES.txt for helm install output (REQ-006)
tasks.description: Create charts/memcached-operator/templates/NOTES.txt with basic post-install instructions: chart name, how to check the operator status (kubectl get pods), and a note about CRD management (crds.managedByHelm). This file is required for helm lint to pass cleanly.

tasks.title: Write helm-unittest test for Chart.yaml metadata and values defaults (REQ-008, REQ-001, REQ-002)
tasks.description: Create charts/memcached-operator/tests/chart_metadata_test.yaml using helm-unittest format. Tests: (1) Chart.yaml has apiVersion v2, type application, name memcached-operator; (2) NOTES.txt is rendered without errors; (3) default values produce expected output. Since this feature only has scaffold files (no deployment template yet), test that helm template with defaults produces NOTES.txt output and that the CRD template is absent by default.

tasks.title: Write helm-unittest test for CRD template toggle (REQ-008, REQ-005)
tasks.description: Create charts/memcached-operator/tests/crd_template_test.yaml using helm-unittest format. Tests: (1) with default values (crds.managedByHelm: false), crd-template.yaml should not render any document; (2) with crds.managedByHelm: true, crd-template.yaml should render a CustomResourceDefinition with correct apiVersion, kind, spec.group=memcached.c5c3.io; (3) when rendered, CRD has standard labels from _helpers.tpl including helm.sh/chart and app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm.

tasks.title: Write helm-unittest test for _helpers.tpl label and name functions (REQ-008, REQ-003)
tasks.description: Create charts/memcached-operator/tests/helpers_test.yaml using helm-unittest format. Test helper output by asserting on labels of the CRD template (enabled via set crds.managedByHelm=true). Tests: (1) app.kubernetes.io/name equals memcached-operator; (2) app.kubernetes.io/instance equals RELEASE-NAME; (3) app.kubernetes.io/managed-by equals Helm; (4) helm.sh/chart contains memcached-operator-0.1.0; (5) with nameOverride set, app.kubernetes.io/name reflects the override; (6) with fullnameOverride set, resource names use the override.

tasks.title: Validate chart with helm lint under multiple configurations (REQ-006)
tasks.description: Run helm lint charts/memcached-operator/ with: (1) default values; (2) all toggles enabled (--set crds.managedByHelm=true,serviceMonitor.enabled=true,networkPolicy.enabled=true); (3) minimal config (--set webhook.enabled=false,certmanager.enabled=false,rbac.create=false). All three must pass with exit code 0 and no ERROR-level messages. Fix any lint issues found.

tasks.title: Write reference documentation for Helm chart scaffold (REQ-010)
tasks.description: Create docs/reference/backend/helm-chart-scaffold.md. Sections: (1) Overview — chart purpose and directory structure; (2) Chart.yaml — fields and versioning strategy (appVersion tracks operator, version is chart version); (3) values.yaml Reference — table of all keys with Key, Type, Default, Description columns; (4) _helpers.tpl Functions — table listing each template function, its purpose, and example output; (5) CRD Handling — explanation of crds/ directory for install vs crds.managedByHelm template for upgrade; (6) .helmignore — explanation of excluded patterns. Follow existing doc style in docs/reference/backend/.

test_specifications.expected: Rendered CRD has helm.sh/chart and app.kubernetes.io/managed-by labels

test_specifications.test_function: Labels include app.kubernetes.io/name set to chart name
test_specifications.story: Template author uses consistent helpers for labels and names

test_specifications.test_function: Labels include app.kubernetes.io/managed-by set to Helm
test_specifications.story: Template author uses consistent helpers for labels and names

test_specifications.test_function: Labels include helm.sh/chart with chart name and version
test_specifications.story: Template author uses consistent helpers for labels and names
Template author uses consistent helpers for labels and names

test_specifications.story: Template author uses consistent helpers for labels and names

review_criteria: Chart.yaml has apiVersion: v2, type: application, name: memcached-operator, valid version and appVersion

values.yaml contains all specified tunables (image, replicaCount, resources, webhook, certmanager, serviceMonitor, networkPolicy, watchNamespaces, leaderElection, rbac, crds, serviceAccount, securityContext) with defaults matching config/ manifests
_helpers.tpl defines name, fullname, chart, labels, and selectorLabels helpers; labels match app.kubernetes.io conventions from config/
crds/ directory contains exact copy of config/crd/bases/memcached.c5c3.io_memcacheds.yaml
templates/crd-template.yaml is correctly gated by {{ if .Values.crds.managedByHelm }} and includes chart labels
helm lint passes with no errors for default values, all-enabled, and minimal configurations
helm-unittest test suites exist and pass for chart metadata, CRD toggle, and helper labels
Reference documentation at docs/reference/backend/helm-chart-scaffold.md covers Chart.yaml, values.yaml keys table, _helpers.tpl functions, and CRD handling strategy
.helmignore file exists with standard exclusion patterns
implementation_notes: This feature creates the foundation chart structure only — no deployment/RBAC/webhook templates yet (those are separate features). The chart must pass helm lint even with just Chart.yaml, values.yaml, _helpers.tpl, NOTES.txt, and the CRD template. Key architectural decisions: (1) CRD dual strategy — crds/ directory for install, optional template for helm-managed upgrade; (2) values.yaml structure must anticipate all downstream template features (deployment, RBAC, webhook, certmanager, servicemonitor, networkpolicy) even though templates don't exist yet; (3) _helpers.tpl label convention matches existing config/ app.kubernetes.io/name: memcached-operator pattern but uses app.kubernetes.io/managed-by: Helm (vs kustomize in Kustomize manifests); (4) Security contexts in values.yaml must mirror config/manager/manager.yaml exactly (runAsNonRoot: true, runAsUser: 65532, seccompProfile RuntimeDefault, readOnlyRootFilesystem: true, drop ALL capabilities); (5) Resource defaults from config/manager/manager.yaml: limits cpu 500m memory 128Mi, requests cpu 10m memory 64Mi.
